metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4592
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2G: Subcellular fractionation followed by Western Blot analysis show that GPR158 is strongly enriched in the Triton-insoluble fraction containing PSD95, suggesting a postsynaptic localization.

Figure 2H: In utero electroporation of embryonic day E15.5 mouse embryos was used to express a GFP reporter and HA-tagged GPR158 (GFP-T2A-HA-GPR158) in CA3 pyramidal neurons and imaged sparsely transduced cells using structured illumination microscopy (SIM) at P21. HA-GPR158 displayed a specific, punctate distribution that was restricted to the proximal portion of GFP-labeled CA3 apical dendrites.

Figure 2I: HA-GPR158 puncta localize to the heads of thorny excrescences. Not all spines display detectable HA immunofluorescence.

Taken together, the results show that GPR158 is enriched in the PSD. Based on the known transmembrane topology of this protein (e.g. PMID: 34793198), "integral component of ... membrane" term was selected.. Experimental description: P21 WT rat brains were homogenized for subcellular fractionation
antibodies: AB_10762838, AB_2113957
GPR158 KO (Gpr158tm1(KOMP)Vlcg) mouse used for antibody specificity control
GFP-T2A-HA-GPR158 construct expressed for SIM microscopy
